Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Microscopes can see video on a laserdisc 435 upvotes, 53 comments
  2. Z80 Sans – a disassembler in a font (2024) 113 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. Artificial-life: A simple (300 lines of code) reproduction of Computational Life 110 upvotes, 12 comments
  4. FrameBook 430 upvotes, 72 comments
  5. Pushing and Pulling: Three reactivity algorithms 99 upvotes, 16 comments
  6. PCB devboard the size of a USB-C plug 174 upvotes, 33 comments
  7. Agent Safehouse – macOS-native sandboxing for local agents 570 upvotes, 141 comments
  8. How the Sriracha guys screwed over their supplier 175 upvotes, 41 comments
  9. Linux Internals: How /proc/self/mem writes to unwritable memory (2021) 80 upvotes, 18 comments
  10. Every single board computer I tested in 2025 167 upvotes, 50 comments
  11. The legendary Mojave Phone Booth is back (2013) 32 upvotes, 6 comments
  12. Case Study: lynnandtonic.com 2025 refresh 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. Show HN: I built a real-time OSINT dashboard pulling 15 live global feeds 238 upvotes, 100 comments
  14. Humanoid robot: The evolution of Kawasaki’s challenge 14 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. US Court of Appeals: TOS may be updated by email, use can imply consent [pdf] 158 upvotes, 70 comments
  16. I made a programming language with M&Ms 80 upvotes, 34 comments
  17. We should revisit literate programming in the agent era 236 upvotes, 139 comments
  18. Show HN: Skir – like Protocol Buffer but better 88 upvotes, 45 comments
  19. I love email (2023) 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  20. WSL Manager 104 upvotes, 55 comments
  21. The death of social media is the renaissance of RSS (2025) 136 upvotes, 76 comments
  22. Show HN: Mcp2cli – One CLI for every API, 96-99% fewer tokens than native MCP 69 upvotes, 36 comments
  23. My Homelab Setup 231 upvotes, 150 comments
  24. Ask HN: How to be alone? 464 upvotes, 336 comments
  25. Why can't you tune your guitar? (2019) 209 upvotes, 148 comments
  26. My “grand vision” for Rust 188 upvotes, 176 comments
  27. Living human brain cells play DOOM on a CL1 [video] 189 upvotes, 186 comments
  28. Grammarly is offering ‘expert’ AI reviews from famous dead and living writers 36 upvotes, 34 comments
  29. Triumph of the toons: how animation came to rule the box office 17 upvotes, 19 comments
  30. Ask HN: What Are You Working On? (March 2026) 147 upvotes, 505 comments

Last generated 6:00pm, 9 March 2026. Source here.
